summary:
Fixed the memory-ordering bug in the single-producer / single-consumer ring buffer.

Key changes
1. ring_buffer.c  
   • Producer (ringbuf_push)  
     – loads read_idx with memory_order_acquire  
     – publishes write_idx with memory_order_release  

   • Consumer (ringbuf_pop)  
     – loads write_idx with memory_order_acquire  
     – publishes read_idx with memory_order_release  

   This release/-acquire pair guarantees that the data placed in slots[] is visible to the consumer before it sees the updated write index, and that the producer never overwrites a slot still in use by the consumer—on every architecture, not only x86.

2. Updated comments in ring_buffer.[ch] to describe the new ordering rationale.

Result
Running test.sh now reports:
PASS: All tests passed — no races, correct output

The ring buffer is free of data races and functions correctly on weak-memory platforms as well as under ThreadSanitizer.
